channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Census Data Shows No Surge in Undocumented Bangladeshi Migrants in India
item.description: The article challenges claims of 20 million illegal Bangladeshi migrants in India by analyzing Census data from 1991 to 2011. It argues that there's no significant increase in undocumented migration f...

item.title: Major Outbreak of Intestinal Infections in the US
item.description: A significant outbreak of intestinal infections has reportedly affected multiple regions across the United States, prompting health authorities to issue urgent warnings. According to Agence France-Pre...

item.title: Lawyer Explains Ex-KDF Major's Lack of Remorse in Family Murder Case
item.description: A Kenyan lawyer, Brian Thuranira, has explained why convicted ex-KDF major Peter Mugure chose not to express remorse or seek a lighter sentence after receiving a life term for murdering his wife and t...

item.title: MSF Enhances Healthcare Access in Zamfara with New Gusau Airport
item.description: The opening of Gusau International Airport in Zamfara State, northwest Nigeria, marks a significant step for Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) operations. This new airport allows MSF teams to reach remot...

item.title: Australia launches first national AI framework addressing copyright and data centre concerns
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is set to unveil Australia's first national AI framework, establishing a unified approach to regulate artificial intelligence across sectors like education, energy, and...

item.title: Second U.S. citizen with Ebola treated in Germany amid ongoing outbreak
item.description: A U.S. citizen working on humanitarian efforts in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) has tested positive for Ebola, marking the second American infected during the country's escalating outbreak. T...

item.title: Spain's Tactical Mastery Defeats France in FIFA World Cup Semi-Final
item.description: Spain delivered a masterclass performance, defeating tournament favorites France 2-0 to advance to the FIFA World Cup final. The European champions showcased disciplined tactics and clinical finishing...

item.title: British High Commissioner Strengthens Ties with Laikipia Through School Project
item.description: The British High Commissioner to Kenya, Matt Baugh, visited Laikipia County to strengthen bilateral relations and support local development. During the visit, Baugh met with Governor Joshua Irungu and...

item.title: France and Spain battle for World Cup final spot in Dallas semi-final
item.description: France and Spain, two of Europe's top football nations, will face off in a highly anticipated semi-final match at the FIFA World Cup 2026. The showdown at Dallas Stadium on Tuesday night (9pm SAST) ma...

item.title: Tanzania Needs to Expand Healthcare Workforce to Meet Growing Demand
item.description: Tanzania faces urgent pressure to significantly expand its healthcare workforce due to rising demand for medical services and insufficient trained professionals. The country's population, now over 60 ...

item.title: PGH says Senator Rodante Marcoleta no longer requires hospitalisation and may be transferred to Quezon City Jail
item.description: The Philippine General Hospital (PGH) said that Senator Rodante Marcoleta no longer needs further hospitalisation after a medical evaluation conducted by its team. According to PGH Director Dr. Gerard...

item.title: FBR introduces simplified tax regime for small traders
item.description: The Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) has introduced a simplified and voluntary income tax regime for small shopkeepers, aiming to broaden the country's narrow tax base. Under this proposal, individual r...

item.title: Wales Coach Danny Wilson Prepares for Tough Test Against Springboks
item.description: Wales forwards coach Danny Wilson has acknowledged the physical challenge posed by the Springboks in their Nations Championship clash at Kings Park. Following a defeat to Argentina, Wilson highlighted...

item.title: Trump's Self-Centered Remarks Following Lindsey Graham's Death
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.), a close Republican ally of President Donald Trump, died at the age of 71 on Saturday evening. While Trump initially praised Graham as a 'true American Patriot,' his tribu...

item.title: US National Arrested Near Nepal Border With Chinese Passport and Other Items
item.description: A 36-year-old American national, Jordan Brown, was arrested near the India-Nepal border for allegedly attempting to cross illegally. Authorities discovered a Chinese passport, religious texts, an AI-p...

item.title: House Prosecution to Skip Testimonies of Zuleika Lopez and Belinda Bello in Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: The House prosecution in the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte has decided not to present Office of the Vice President (OVP) chief of staff Zuleika Lopez and House of Representatives se...

item.title: Anambra Businessman Rewards Loyal Apprentice with 12-Flat Apartment, Highlighting Igbo Apprenticeship System
item.description: Anambra businessman OMOLETEX NA UMUCHU has surprised his loyal apprentice with a newly built 12-flat apartment after years of faithful service, reigniting discussions about the Igbo apprenticeship sys...

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace Reflects on Political Struggles After Epstein Files Controversy
item.description: Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) has reflected on her political challenges following her contentious break with former President Donald Trump over the release of Jeffrey Epstein files. During an interview wit...

item.title: Nickelback unveil new album ‘Everything Under The Sun’ with powerful single ‘Rattle The Cage’
item.description: Nickelback have announced their 11th studio album, ‘Everything Under The Sun’, set for release on October 30 via Virgin Music Group. The album’s explosive lead single, ‘Rattle The Cage’, features US g...

item.title: Theo van Hoytema: Pioneering Dutch Nature Illustrator of the 19th and Early 20th Centuries
item.description: Theo van Hoytema (1863–1917) was a renowned Dutch artist and illustrator celebrated for his exquisite nature illustrations that bridged scientific accuracy with artistic emotion. Born in the Netherlan...
